what_it_does: Provides the CSS for the ViIdentity overlay application surface, adding the idy-* shell and projection classes that style a three-pane identity dashboard inside the ViMemory panel. It covers layout, navigation rail, aspect detail views, a persistent Vi conversation column, editable facets, recall and behaviour CRUD, console/teach/produce flows, generated candidate cards with provenance traces, a network graph, and an overview dashboard.
observations: Uses an idy-* class namespace and BEM-style block-element-modifier naming throughout.
Builds a three-pane layout: idy-rail (248px sidebar), idy-detail (flex main), and idy-vi (340px persistent conversation column).
Relies entirely on external CSS custom properties such as --accent-gold, --bg-canvas, --s-5, --r-xl, and --fs-sm.
Contains many interactive state classes: .on, .idy-facet--on, .idy-made__card--approved, .idy-made__card--rejected, hover states, and disabled states.
Includes inline-editing UI patterns with pen icons, textarea editors, and save/cancel footers for facets and behaviours.
Defines a network graph visual with absolute-positioned nodes and an SVG line layer using dashed gold strokes.
Has a responsive breakpoint at 920px that collapses the three-pane layout into a stacked vertical layout.
References keyframe animations voice-fade, idy-spin, and idy-pop, but only defines idy-spin and idy-pop in this file.
Provides provenance/decision UI via .idy-trace with source tags (preference, explicit, memory, ai, default) and lean chips.
Includes a home/overview section (idy-ov) with tiles, recent items, leans, and quick actions.
Uses a warm gold accent palette for Vi-branded surfaces and a muted bronze for secondary accents.
standouts: The persistent Vi conversation column (idy-vi) is styled as a golden sidebar that floats alongside every aspect.
The network graph (idy-net) is rendered with absolute-positioned nodes and dashed SVG lines, including a central core node.
Generated candidate cards (idy-made) include provenance traces and approve/reject decision controls.
Inline CRUD patterns for facets, recall chips, and behaviours are encoded directly in CSS.
conventions: CSS custom-property-driven theming with local fallbacks (e.g., var(--fs-sm, 13px)).
Spacing and sizing tokens via var(--s-*) and var(--r-*) rather than raw values.
Status and semantic color tokens such as --status-success, --status-error, --accent-gold-ink.
Modifier classes like .on, --gold, --live, and --off to indicate state.
Section comments that split the file into layout, operate, produce, and home areas.
A single max-width media query for tablet/mobile adaptation.
concerns: Many selectors depend on classes defined in other stylesheets, so this file is not self-contained.
The voice-fade animation is referenced by .id-panel but not defined in this file.
Some fixed widths (248px rail, 340px Vi column) may constrain narrow viewports even before the breakpoint.
A few rules use !important (e.g., .idy-recall__forget:hover), which can complicate overrides.
The file is large and mixes layout, components, interactions, and animations, increasing maintenance surface.
Hardcoded pixel values appear alongside token values in some places.
notes: A single, dense stylesheet that turns the ViIdentity overlay into a full editable dashboard with its own layout, conversation column, and produce/teach flows.
questions: Where are the consumed CSS custom properties (e.g., --accent-gold-50, --s-5, --r-xl) actually defined?
Which JavaScript or framework components map to the idy-* class names?
How are the network graph node positions and connecting lines generated or updated?
Where is the voice-fade keyframe animation defined?
What component owns the .mem-aspect, .dsys-spec, .sp, and .strm classes referenced here?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it adds the idy-* shell and projection styling for a live mirror-application identity overlay hosted inside ViMemory, reusing external component classes and covering all described aspects.
apparent_themes: Identity as an inspectable, editable self-model
Persistent conversational companion alongside every aspect
Provenance and decision transparency for generated outputs
intention_signals: The stylesheet appears to support a visual identity editor where a user can explore Vi's composed mind, adjust preferences and behaviours, review generated candidates with traceable provenance, and chat with Vi from any aspect.
novelty: It encodes an entire interactive identity-editing application surface in one CSS file, including inline editing, decision provenance traces, a network graph, and a persistent AI companion column.
contribution: The complete visual vocabulary and layout rules for the ViIdentity overlay, giving the fused system a consistent, editable, aspect-driven identity interface.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet defining the idy-* design system for the ViIdentity overlay, including layout shells, navigation, detail views, a persistent Vi conversation column, editable identity facets, recall/behaviour CRUD, console/teach/produce flows, generated candidate cards, a network graph, and an overview dashboard.
intention_for_embedding: To style a live, editable mirror-application for Vi's identity inside the ViMemory overlay, enabling users to inspect, tune, and converse with Vi across multiple aspects.
